# parse

```go
import "text/template/parse"
```

Package parse builds parse trees for templates as defined by text/template and html/template. Clients should use those packages to construct templates rather than this one, which provides shared internal data structures not intended for general use.

<a name="IsEmptyTree"></a>

## func [IsEmptyTree](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/parse.go#L283>)

```go
func IsEmptyTree(n Node) bool
```

IsEmptyTree reports whether this tree \(node\) is empty of everything but space or comments.

<a name="Parse"></a>

## func [Parse](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/parse.go#L74>)

```go
func Parse(name, text, leftDelim, rightDelim string, funcs ...map[string]any) (map[string]*Tree, error)
```

Parse returns a map from template name to [Tree](<#Tree>), created by parsing the templates described in the argument string. The top\-level template will be given the specified name. If an error is encountered, parsing stops and an empty map is returned with the error.

<a name="ActionNode"></a>

## type [ActionNode](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L261-L267>)

ActionNode holds an action \(something bounded by delimiters\). Control actions have their own nodes; ActionNode represents simple ones such as field evaluations and parenthesized pipelines.

```go
type ActionNode struct {
    NodeType
    Pos

    Line int       // The line number in the input. Deprecated: Kept for compatibility.
    Pipe *PipeNode // The pipeline in the action.
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="BranchNode"></a>

## type [BranchNode](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L844-L852>)

BranchNode is the common representation of if, range, and with.

```go
type BranchNode struct {
    NodeType
    Pos

    Line     int       // The line number in the input. Deprecated: Kept for compatibility.
    Pipe     *PipeNode // The pipeline to be evaluated.
    List     *ListNode // What to execute if the value is non-empty.
    ElseList *ListNode // What to execute if the value is empty (nil if absent).
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="ChainNode"></a>

## type [ChainNode](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L532-L538>)

ChainNode holds a term followed by a chain of field accesses \(identifier starting with '.'\). The names may be chained \('.x.y'\). The periods are dropped from each ident.

```go
type ChainNode struct {
    NodeType
    Pos

    Node  Node
    Field []string // The identifiers in lexical order.
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="ChainNode.Add"></a>

### func \(\*ChainNode\) [Add](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L545>)

```go
func (c *ChainNode) Add(field string)
```

Add adds the named field \(which should start with a period\) to the end of the chain.

<a name="IdentifierNode.SetPos"></a>

### func \(\*IdentifierNode\) [SetPos](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L361>)

```go
func (i *IdentifierNode) SetPos(pos Pos) *IdentifierNode
```

SetPos sets the position. [NewIdentifier](<#NewIdentifier>) is a public method so we can't modify its signature. Chained for convenience. TODO: fix one day?

<a name="IdentifierNode.SetTree"></a>

### func \(\*IdentifierNode\) [SetTree](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L369>)

```go
func (i *IdentifierNode) SetTree(t *Tree) *IdentifierNode
```

SetTree sets the parent tree for the node. [NewIdentifier](<#NewIdentifier>) is a public method so we can't modify its signature. Chained for convenience. TODO: fix one day?

<a name="Mode"></a>

## type [Mode](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/parse.go#L39>)

A Mode value is a set of flags \(or 0\). Modes control parser behavior.

```go
type Mode uint
```

<a name="ParseComments"></a>

```go
const (
    ParseComments Mode = 1 << iota // parse comments and add them to AST
    SkipFuncCheck                  // do not check that functions are defined
)
```

<a name="Node"></a>

## type [Node](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L20-L33>)

A Node is an element in the parse tree. The interface is trivial. The interface contains an unexported method so that only types local to this package can satisfy it.

```go
type Node interface {
    Type() NodeType
    String() string
    // Copy does a deep copy of the Node and all its components.
    // To avoid type assertions, some XxxNodes also have specialized
    // CopyXxx methods that return *XxxNode.
    Copy() Node
    Position() Pos // byte position of start of node in full original input string
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

<a name="NodeType"></a>

## type [NodeType](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L36>)

NodeType identifies the type of a parse tree node.

```go
type NodeType int
```

<a name="NodeText"></a>

```go
const (
    NodeText    NodeType = iota // Plain text.
    NodeAction                  // A non-control action such as a field evaluation.
    NodeBool                    // A boolean constant.
    NodeChain                   // A sequence of field accesses.
    NodeCommand                 // An element of a pipeline.
    NodeDot                     // The cursor, dot.

    NodeField      // A field or method name.
    NodeIdentifier // An identifier; always a function name.
    NodeIf         // An if action.
    NodeList       // A list of Nodes.
    NodeNil        // An untyped nil constant.
    NodeNumber     // A numerical constant.
    NodePipe       // A pipeline of commands.
    NodeRange      // A range action.
    NodeString     // A string constant.
    NodeTemplate   // A template invocation action.
    NodeVariable   // A $ variable.
    NodeWith       // A with action.
    NodeComment    // A comment.
    NodeBreak      // A break action.
    NodeContinue   // A continue action.
)
```

<a name="NodeType.Type"></a>

### func \(NodeType\) [Type](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L48>)

```go
func (t NodeType) Type() NodeType
```

Type returns itself and provides an easy default implementation for embedding in a Node. Embedded in all non\-trivial Nodes.

<a name="NumberNode"></a>

## type [NumberNode](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L618-L631>)

NumberNode holds a number: signed or unsigned integer, float, or complex. The value is parsed and stored under all the types that can represent the value. This simulates in a small amount of code the behavior of Go's ideal constants.

```go
type NumberNode struct {
    NodeType
    Pos

    IsInt      bool       // Number has an integral value.
    IsUint     bool       // Number has an unsigned integral value.
    IsFloat    bool       // Number has a floating-point value.
    IsComplex  bool       // Number is complex.
    Int64      int64      // The signed integer value.
    Uint64     uint64     // The unsigned integer value.
    Float64    float64    // The floating-point value.
    Complex128 complex128 // The complex value.
    Text       string     // The original textual representation from the input.
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NumberNode.Copy"></a>

### func \(\*NumberNode\) [Copy](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L748>)

```go
func (n *NumberNode) Copy() Node
```

<a name="NumberNode.String"></a>

### func \(\*NumberNode\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L736>)

```go
func (n *NumberNode) String() string
```

<a name="PipeNode"></a>

## type [PipeNode](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L188-L196>)

PipeNode holds a pipeline with optional declaration

```go
type PipeNode struct {
    NodeType
    Pos

    Line     int             // The line number in the input. Deprecated: Kept for compatibility.
    IsAssign bool            // The variables are being assigned, not declared.
    Decl     []*VariableNode // Variables in lexical order.
    Cmds     []*CommandNode  // The commands in lexical order.
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

func (p *PipeNode) CopyPipe() *PipeNode

## type [Pos](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L40>)

Pos represents a byte position in the original input text from which this template was parsed.

```go
type Pos int
```

RangeNode represents a \{\{range\}\} action and its commands.

```go
type RangeNode struct {
    BranchNode
}
```

<a name="StringNode"></a>

## type [StringNode](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L755-L761>)

StringNode holds a string constant. The value has been "unquoted".

```go
type StringNode struct {
    NodeType
    Pos

    Quoted string // The original text of the string, with quotes.
    Text   string // The string, after quote processing.
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="TemplateNode"></a>

## type [TemplateNode](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L976-L983>)

TemplateNode represents a \{\{template\}\} action.

```go
type TemplateNode struct {
    NodeType
    Pos

    Line int       // The line number in the input. Deprecated: Kept for compatibility.
    Name string    // The name of the template (unquoted).
    Pipe *PipeNode // The command to evaluate as dot for the template.
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

TextNode holds plain text.

}

<a name="Tree"></a>

## type [Tree](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/parse.go#L20-L36>)

Tree is the representation of a single parsed template.

```go
type Tree struct {
    Name      string    // name of the template represented by the tree.
    ParseName string    // name of the top-level template during parsing, for error messages.
    Root      *ListNode // top-level root of the tree.
    Mode      Mode      // parsing mode.
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>

### func [New](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/parse.go#L143>)

```go
func New(name string, funcs ...map[string]any) *Tree
```

New allocates a new parse tree with the given name.

<a name="Tree.Copy"></a>

### func \(\*Tree\) [Copy](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/parse.go#L58>)

```go
func (t *Tree) Copy() *Tree
```

Copy returns a copy of the [Tree](<#Tree>). Any parsing state is discarded.

<a name="Tree.ErrorContext"></a>

### func \(\*Tree\) [ErrorContext](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/parse.go#L153>)

```go
func (t *Tree) ErrorContext(n Node) (location, context string)
```

ErrorContext returns a textual representation of the location of the node in the input text. The receiver is only used when the node does not have a pointer to the tree inside, which can occur in old code.

<a name="Tree.Parse"></a>

### func \(\*Tree\) [Parse](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/parse.go#L258>)

```go
func (t *Tree) Parse(text, leftDelim, rightDelim string, treeSet map[string]*Tree, funcs ...map[string]any) (tree *Tree, err error)
```

Parse parses the template definition string to construct a representation of the template for execution. If either action delimiter string is empty, the default \("\{\{" or "\}\}"\) is used. Embedded template definitions are added to the treeSet map.

<a name="VariableNode"></a>

## type [VariableNode](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/text/template/parse/node.go#L392-L397>)

VariableNode holds a list of variable names, possibly with chained field accesses. The dollar sign is part of the \(first\) name.

```go
type VariableNode struct {
    NodeType
    Pos

    Ident []string // Variable name and fields in lexical order.
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```
